rationale: The citing paper uses Chung and Narayandas (2017) as a specific prior study in its core literature framing, not just a background list item. In Table 1, it explicitly contrasts that study's disincentive (loss of a gifted quota bonus) with the current paper's harsher disincentive (threat of job loss with trained replacements), and this distinction helps motivate the new hypotheses and contribution. This citation therefore plays a meaningful positioning role in defining how the bench program differs from prior sales compensation work.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the line of sales force compensation research represented by Chung and Narayandas (2017) by examining a different and more severe deterrence mechanism: a credible termination threat reinforced by visible replacements. It builds on that prior disincentive-related evidence to motivate a new context and mechanism for improving laggard performance.
